entities.title: Knowledge Hub Foundations
entities.subtitle: Editorial Collection
entities.summary: Knowledge Hub Foundations curates the first expansion wave of Electronic Artefacts knowledge records around graph systems, preservation, generative systems, signal, audio and public semantic publishing.
entities.description: A curated collection grouping foundational Knowledge Hub concepts, technologies, programs, projects and publications.

entities.title: Contextual Execution
entities.summary: Contextual execution describes runtime behavior where an operation is evaluated through entity identity, graph relations, permissions, temporal state and intended scope.
entities.description: A canonical Electronic Artefacts concept connecting graph runtime, Runtime Theory, VASTE and identity-aware systems.
entities.definition: Contextual execution is the practice of making identity, state, permissions, relationships and operating context active constraints on what a system can do.

entities.title: Creative Coding
entities.summary: Creative coding connects software, visual culture, sound, interaction, generative systems, education and experimental publishing.
entities.description: A canonical definition of creative coding for Electronic Artefacts knowledge articles, projects and programs.
entities.definition: Creative coding is the use of programming as an artistic, design or exploratory medium rather than only as an engineering implementation tool.

entities.title: Cybernetic Feedback
entities.summary: Cybernetic feedback connects control, communication, systems theory, generative art, machine listening, adaptive interfaces and runtime governance.
entities.description: A canonical definition of cybernetic feedback for Electronic Artefacts research, creative systems and historical technology context.
entities.definition: Cybernetic feedback is the circulation of information about a system's state back into the system so that behavior can be regulated, adapted or transformed.

entities.title: Digital Preservation
entities.summary: Digital preservation connects file formats, metadata, provenance, storage, rights, migration and interpretation into a long-term care practice for digital culture.
entities.description: A canonical definition of digital preservation for Electronic Artefacts archives, Palimpsests, Vestiges and knowledge infrastructure.
entities.definition: Digital preservation is the long-term work of keeping digital objects understandable, authentic, usable and accessible despite changes in formats, software, hardware and context.

entities.title: Entity Identity
entities.summary: Entity identity gives knowledge systems a way to cite things without confusing names, pages, files, versions and changing descriptions.
entities.description: A canonical definition of entity identity for graph publishing, semantic URLs, archives and Electronic Artefacts records.
entities.definition: Entity identity is the durable naming of a person, concept, project, program, publication or artefact so that references remain stable as descriptions evolve.

entities.title: Generative System
entities.summary: Generative systems connect creative coding, cybernetics, algorithmic composition, simulation, design tools and AI by shifting authorship toward rules, feedback and selection.
entities.description: A canonical definition of generative system for creative coding, sound design, design automation and Electronic Artefacts projects.
entities.definition: A generative system is a rule-based, procedural or computational arrangement that produces variable outputs from defined constraints, materials and processes.

entities.title: Graph Runtime
entities.summary: Electronic Artefacts uses graph runtime to describe systems where entities, relationships, identity, context and events form an executable structure rather than a passive data model.
entities.description: A canonical definition of graph runtime, its scope, applications and relationship to VASTE, Runtime Theory and Vestiges.
entities.definition: A graph runtime is an execution environment in which addressable entities and typed relationships participate directly in contextual computation.

entities.title: Knowledge Graph
entities.summary: Electronic Artefacts uses knowledge graph to describe public knowledge systems where concepts, projects, programs, publications, sources and artefacts keep stable identities and typed relations.
entities.description: A canonical definition of knowledge graph for cultural, technical and research publishing.
entities.definition: A knowledge graph is a structured network of entities, identifiers and typed relationships that makes knowledge navigable, queryable and reusable across contexts.

entities.title: Linked Data
entities.summary: Linked Data gives public knowledge systems a way to make entities, pages, references and datasets interoperable across sites and software systems.
entities.description: A canonical definition of Linked Data as it applies to Electronic Artefacts, JSON-LD, RDF, SEO and public knowledge pages.
entities.definition: Linked Data is a web publishing approach that identifies things with stable URIs and connects them through machine-readable statements.

entities.title: Provenance
entities.summary: Provenance helps readers assess reliability, rights, authorship, lineage, context and trust across research, archive, software and cultural records.
entities.description: A canonical definition of provenance for Electronic Artefacts publications, artefacts, archives and graph relations.
entities.definition: Provenance is information about the people, activities, sources, transformations and decisions involved in producing a thing or piece of data.

entities.title: Signal Archaeology
entities.summary: Signal archaeology gives Electronic Artefacts a way to connect audio analysis, media residue, archival provenance, memory and cultural interpretation.
entities.description: A canonical Electronic Artefacts concept linking ORETH, Palimpsests, digital preservation, provenance and machine listening.
entities.definition: Signal archaeology is the interpretation of technical, sonic, visual or archival traces as evidence of hidden processes, transformations and histories.

entities.title: Electronic Artefacts
entities.subtitle: Studio / label / research
entities.summary: Electronic Artefacts is an independent creative technology studio, software laboratory, research organization, cultural platform, label and publishing system.
entities.description: Electronic Artefacts connects research, software, design, cultural production and archival practice through a shared graph of projects, programs and knowledge.

entities.title: ORETH
entities.subtitle: Audio Intelligence Program
entities.summary: ORETH is an Electronic Artefacts audio intelligence and artistic research program concerned with signal analysis, pattern detection, memory, machine listening and experimental music production.
entities.description: A public program record for ORETH as both an audio research system and the technical-cultural layer connected to Palimpsests.

entities.title: VASTE
entities.subtitle: Proprietary Runtime
entities.summary: VASTE is the primary proprietary runtime developed and commercialized by Electronic Artefacts for graph architectures, contextual execution, identity systems and modular world construction.
entities.description: VASTE connects Runtime Theory to implemented graph systems and can be evaluated through scoped repository access, technical pilots, implementation work and licensing conversations.

entities.title: Palimpsests
entities.subtitle: Conceptual Audio Publication
entities.summary: Palimpsests is an Electronic Artefacts cultural project and conceptual album cycle connected to ORETH, memory, inheritance, transmission, entropy, signal and layered artistic production.
entities.description: A canonical project record for Palimpsests as a compound audio, visual and research publication within Electronic Artefacts.

entities.title: Vestiges
entities.subtitle: VASTE Flagship Knowledge Platform
entities.summary: Vestiges is a VASTE-powered knowledge infrastructure platform designed to map, preserve, connect, transmit and activate human know-how.
entities.description: The project applies graph-runtime architecture to culture, craft, institutions, materials, techniques, places and public knowledge pages.

entities.title: Contextual Execution and Graph Runtimes
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains contextual execution as a runtime principle for systems where identity, relations, permissions and state influence what operations can occur.
entities.description: A technical article connecting contextual execution, graph runtime, Runtime Theory and VASTE.

entities.title: Digital Preservation and Living Archives
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains digital preservation as an active cultural practice that keeps digital objects, metadata, provenance and interpretation usable over time.
entities.description: A technical article on digital preservation, living archives, file formats, provenance and Electronic Artefacts cultural records.

entities.title: Foundational Lineage #001
entities.subtitle: Research Note
entities.summary: This research note records early architecture, communication-system and documentation threads that led to Electronic Artefacts, Runtime Theory and VASTE.
entities.description: A concise record of the foundational research lineage connecting information architecture, taxonomy, runtime systems and public knowledge structures.

entities.title: Generative Systems, Cybernetics and Creative Coding
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article traces generative systems from cybernetic feedback and algorithmic art to creative coding, sound design and contemporary AI-assisted production.
entities.description: A technical article on generative systems, cybernetic feedback, creative coding, algorithmic composition and Electronic Artefacts projects.

entities.title: Knowledge Graphs for Cultural Infrastructure
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains how knowledge graphs support cultural infrastructure by giving people, works, sources, concepts, projects and archives stable identities and explicit relations.
entities.description: A technical article on knowledge graphs, cultural heritage, provenance and public knowledge pages, connected to Vestiges and the Electronic Artefacts graph.

entities.title: Linked Data and Public Knowledge Pages
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains how public knowledge pages can use stable URLs, JSON-LD, RDF-oriented identifiers and internal links to become useful to humans, search engines and AI systems.
entities.description: A technical article on Linked Data, JSON-LD, canonical URLs, identifiers and SEO for static knowledge platforms.

entities.title: Signal Archaeology, Audio Memory and Machine Listening
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains signal archaeology as a way to read sonic and technical traces through audio analysis, provenance, memory and machine listening.
entities.description: A technical article on signal archaeology, audio memory, machine listening, ORETH and Palimpsests.

entities.title: Runtime Theory
entities.subtitle: Research field
entities.summary: Runtime Theory investigates the minimum identities, relations, contexts and event primitives required for a coherent executable system.
entities.description: An active Electronic Artefacts research field connecting early VOID experiments, VASTE and the definition of graph runtime.

entities.title: JSON-LD
entities.subtitle: JSON for Linked Data
entities.summary: JSON-LD is a W3C format for serializing Linked Data in JSON, making semantic graph metadata compatible with common web programming workflows.
entities.description: A technology record for JSON-LD as the structured data format used by Electronic Artefacts canonical pages.

entities.title: RDF
entities.subtitle: Resource Description Framework
entities.summary: RDF is a W3C graph data model for representing information as subject-predicate-object statements about resources.
entities.description: A technology record for RDF as a reference model behind knowledge graphs, Linked Data and semantic publication.

relations.subject: ea:publication:digital-preservation-and-living-archives

relations.subject: ea:publication:digital-preservation-and-living-archives
